name: already-resolved
description: Error rate was 15% between 10:00-11:00 but dropped back to 1.2% since 11:30. Self-resolved.

item.title: Error rate spike on customer-support-bot
item.description: Automated alert: error rate exceeded 10% threshold on customer-support-bot between 10:00-11:00 UTC.
item.note:
Investigated the error rate spike. Timeline:

- 10:00-11:00 UTC: error rate was 15.2% (12 of 79 traces failed)
- All 12 errors were "rate_limit_exceeded" from OpenAI (HTTP 429)
- Errors clustered between 10:15-10:45 UTC, coinciding with a burst of 40 traces in 15 minutes (vs normal ~8/hr)
- 11:00-11:30 UTC: error rate dropped to 3.1% (2 of 65 traces)
- 11:30 UTC onwards: error rate back to 1.2% (1 of 82 traces) — within baseline
- Current rate at 13:00 UTC: 0.9% over last hour
- No config changes or deployments during the window
- The burst pattern suggests a batch job or automated client hitting the endpoint
- trace-ids for sample errors: res-4401, res-4412, res-4419

The issue has self-resolved. The rate limiting was transient and tied to a traffic burst. Current error rate is back within the <2% baseline.

projectMemory:
## Project Agents
- **customer-support-bot**: Handles customer inquiries via RAG. Typical trace has 3-5 LLM calls, 1-2 retrieval steps. Normal latency: 2-4s per trace.
- **email-classifier**: Classifies incoming emails. Single LLM call per trace, ~500ms.

## Baselines
- Error rate: <2%
- Daily cost: ~$5
- Trace volume: ~200/day
